calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs (Successor Trustee).
Sara Taylor for Pauline White (Conservator of the Person).

Ms. Navia stated that she filed a response to the objections, that they discussed how to rectify the issue and that they have agreed to reduce the reserve amount to $2,000.00. Ms. Navia also stated that there are no objections to the additional fees for the Trustee or the Attorney, and provided a revised form of order that all parties agreed to. The Court granted the Seventh and Final Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-05-287042 Conservatorship of Peter Fiori and PTR-06-288417 The Peter Fiori Trust

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Paula Bibbs (Conservator of the Estate). Gregory O'Keeffe for Pauline White (Conservator of the Person).

Ms. Gordon explained that the Conservatee was moved from his home to the Sutro Heights care facility in 2011, and then moved to the Aegis care facility in 2012 on an emergeny basis as a result of the Conservatee's agressive behavior, and that they were not advised of the move. The Court expressed concern about there not being enough funds to provide for the Conservatee's care needs. Ms. Bibbs stated that there are enough funds for one to one and a half years or so and then they will apply for Medi Cal, and that Aegis accepts Medi Cal. Ms. Bibbs also explained services provided to support her request for fees. The Court advised Mr. O'Keefe that Ms. White needs to communicate with Ms. Bibbs. Both accounting petitions were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for Paula Bibbs.

The Court stated that the fees requested were high. Ms. Gordon stated that Ms. Bibbs dealt with problems with the credit union, dealt with the financial institution, managed the accounts and that there were accounts for the conservatorship and the trust. In addition, Ms. Gordon stated that the Trustee did the accountings and that $1,000.00 per year was not extra-ordinary. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Janet Pond, CSR#5292. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
